summary:
Agent concluded based on reviewed literature that among listed options, only "Extensor hallucis longus" is a correct answer as it primarily functions to extend toes instead of invert feet; all others play significant roles directly or indirectly aiding in foot inversion movements such as those performed via actions from Tibilias Anterior/Posterior & Peroneus Longus which lift/arch stabilize while also assisting inversions when positioned correctly during gait cycles etc.. To clarify further if needed please ask! Let me know how I can improve your understanding here too. Your feedback helps refine future responses effectively tailored towards clarity/conciseness goals outlined earlier regarding requirements specified above. Thank you!
The primary muscles responsible for foot inversion include the tibialis anterior and posteriors along with some contribution potentially coming through dynamic interactions involving structures
